description: Sample deck for the frozen ppt-maker workflow with Thank You followed by appendix Mermaid diagrams.

slides.title: DALP for Tokenized Metals
slides.subtitle: Middle East executive briefing | frozen workflow example

slides.title: Market case
slides.subtitle: Gold programs need more than a token mint

slides.title: Why metals are live now
slides.body:
• Precious metals already have recognized custody, valuation, and investor demand, so tokenization starts from a credible asset story rather than a speculative one.
• The hard part is operational: linking issuance, holder eligibility, custody facts, and redemption rules without creating spreadsheet-driven side processes.
• DALP matters here because the platform turns those controls into governed configuration instead of one-off contract work.
• That gives issuers a path to launch one program, prove it, and reuse the model across future assets.

slides.title: Product evidence matters
slides.body: This is the point: precious metals are represented in DALP as a supported asset class inside the same lifecycle environment as other regulated products. That signals reuse, governance, and operating discipline instead of a one-off commodity demo.

slides.title: Gold-program needs vs DALP
slides.body: A credible metals platform has to connect physical-asset facts, eligible distribution, lifecycle controls, and day-two operations. DALP maps to that operating need directly rather than treating the token as the whole product.
table_data: Gold is not a generic token

slides.title: Operating model
slides.subtitle: Configuration, controls, and servicing on one plane

slides.title: What has to work together
slides.col1:
Issuance
• Define product terms with explicit metal, unit, and redemption choices.
• Configure supply, metadata, and distribution parameters before launch.
• Launch from governed templates instead of one-off smart-contract work.
• Keep approval readiness visible before the programme goes live.
slides.col2:
Controls
• Bind identity, wallet permissions, and investor claims together.
• Enforce jurisdiction, whitelist, and holding logic before transfer.
• Separate maker, checker, and operator actions for governance.
• Keep policy decisions observable when an exception needs review.
slides.col3:
Servicing
• Run redemptions, freezes, and status changes on-platform.
• Monitor events, reconciliation points, and operational exceptions.
• Preserve audit-ready evidence for partners and internal review.
• Reuse the same servicing pattern across future asset launches.

slides.title: Compliance is part of setup
slides.body: DALP exposes compliance and permissioning in the operating workflow itself. That matters for metals because transfer control, jurisdiction logic, and exception handling cannot live as manual review steps beside the platform.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 6 - Compliance Modules.jpg

slides.title: Why SettleMint wins
slides.body:
• DALP gives institutions a governed way to design, launch, and operate digital assets instead of stitching together isolated tooling.
• For precious metals, that means the token, the eligibility logic, the servicing workflow, and the operating evidence can stay connected.
• That is the difference between a flashy pilot and something a bank, exchange, or custodian can actually stand behind.
• The commercial story is simple: launch faster, prove control earlier, and reuse the same operating model across adjacent products.

slides.title: Appendix
slides.subtitle: Reference diagrams for the operating model

slides.title: Metals lifecycle overview
slides.body: This appendix view shows the end-to-end precious-metals programme lifecycle from setup through redemption and settlement. It is intentionally more detailed than a main-slide visual because the goal here is reference value: where configuration happens, when policy attaches, where issuance begins, how servicing continues after launch, and how redemption closes the loop back to the underlying asset record.

slides.title: Compliance transfer sequence
slides.body: The transfer sequence highlights how identity checks, policy evaluation, blockchain execution, and custody-side events line up for a controlled transfer or redemption path. In a metals context, that matters because an apparently simple movement can still require eligibility review, audit evidence, and a downstream custody action before the business process is truly complete.

slides.title: Deployment roadmap
slides.body: A metals program usually moves from scoping into configuration, integration, readiness, pilot, and rollout. This appendix timeline is there to support planning conversations.

slides.title: Platform topology
slides.body: This appendix topology shows the relationship between channels, DALP business services, execution components, and the external control points around custody and settlement.

slides.title: Data and control model
slides.body: The underlying data model ties programme definitions, assets, policies, investors, identity records, holdings, and events together so operational evidence stays attached to the product record. That linkage is what makes reporting, exception analysis, and controls review credible once the programme is live and servicing activity starts to accumulate over time.
